meta.changelog: v2.1 -- Removed hardcoded action instructions from deadline notes and technology fields. Contacts remain as data. Agents reason from context, not from embedded directives.

mission.statement: KAI-Netics is building a business that provides for Kevin's family today and leaves a legacy for his grandson MONK tomorrow. Starting at net zero, bootstrapped, lean, and growth-focused. The short game funds the long game. The long game builds the platform. The platform builds the legacy.
mission.founder_voice: I want to build a business that will take care of my expenses, support my family, and leave a legacy for my grandson. I need to put myself to work and start with a bootstrap model -- very lean but growth focused. I need to engage with the long money for long term success and I need small wins and experience all around. I need to have short game, long game, and longer game building on each other and I need to look like a strong long term outfit.

legacy.name: MONK
legacy.note: Kevin's grandson. The platform, the IP holding company, and the hardware trust layer are all named in his honor. Everything built here is ultimately for him.

short_game.objective: Personal income. Kevin on the ground delivering personally. First inspection contracts under Part 107. OMB M-26-02 compliance consulting. Bootstrap everything. No overhead that does not generate revenue.
short_game.success_metric: Livable wage achieved. First contract signed. First flight logged commercially.
key_actions: OMB M-26-02 consulting -- CISSP + Part 107 -- zero flight hours required -- May 20 deadline
First infrastructure inspection contract -- utility, bridge, or highway -- DMV
BGE supplier registration and T&D outreach -- fleet arrives July 1
SAM.gov UEI confirmed -- gates all federal work
IRS Form 1023 filed -- gates all grant funding

long_game.objective: EAF cohorts producing certified operators. Fleet growing. Managed service contracts replacing one-off work. MRR building toward $30K-46K/month. MONK licensing conversations starting. Grant funding subsidizing EAF operating costs.
long_game.success_metric: CAKO Cohort 1 complete. First managed service SLA signed. First grant awarded.
key_actions: First MRR contract -- UMMS or BGE

longer_game.objective: CAKO graduates employed across the DMV. KAI-Netics as the infrastructure drone operator for Maryland. MONK as a licensed technology standard. AKMS data moat generating licensing revenue. Legacy built for MONK.
longer_game.success_metric: 50+ CAKO graduates employed. $5M+ ARR. MONK licensed to 3+ OEM partners.
key_actions: Part 135 certificate -- medical and logistics delivery
AKMS data licensing -- proprietary flight dataset

phase.narrative: KAI-Netics is in its first operational month. The platform is live. All agents are running. The legal entities are established. Kevin holds active FAA Part 107 certification and a deep credential stack. No revenue has been earned yet. The immediate priority is first dollar -- OMB M-26-02 compliance consulting requires zero flight hours and closes in 19 days. The first infrastructure inspection contract requires building flight proficiency in parallel with BD outreach. The EAF 501c3 determination is the critical path item blocking all grant funding. Every action this month either generates income or unblocks the next income source.
priority_stack: 1. OMB M-26-02 compliance consulting -- first dollar, no drone required, May 20 deadline
2. IRS Form 1023 filing -- unblocks all grant funding
3. SAM.gov UEI confirmation -- unblocks all federal contracting
4. BGE outreach -- first inspection contract -- fleet arrives July 1
5. Flight hours -- build proficiency in parallel, not as a blocker
6. CAKO pre-cohort -- identify first students while compliance is being built
phase.first_dollar_path: OMB M-26-02 federal drone compliance consulting. Every federal contractor operating drones must comply by May 20, 2026. Kevin's CISSP + Part 107 combination is uniquely qualified to audit and advise. No flight hours required. This is a consulting engagement, not an inspection service. Rate: $150-250/hour or $5,000-15,000 fixed engagement.

founder.name: Kevin Alexander Smith
founder.background: 33-year IT veteran. Medical supply chain insider knowledge from Medline, MedImmune, MedStar. Enterprise career spanning healthcare, financial services (Capital One), and federal (Microsoft). UMGC current student. DMV region based.
roles: FAA Part 107 Operator -- active, seeking employment and contracts

cako_workforce_pipeline.description: CAKO graduates are not just trainees -- they are KAI-Netics' talent supply chain. When contracts are won, CAKO graduates fill the operator roles. When graduates are trained, contracts must exist to place them. The business feeds itself when both sides are active.
cako_workforce_pipeline.temp_agency_model: Kevin runs a drone operator staffing function -- placing CAKO graduates with infrastructure clients, DFR programs, and managed service accounts. Kevin must know the frontlines and deliver the jobs. This is both a revenue stream and a workforce development outcome.
cako_workforce_pipeline.operator_pipeline: Each cohort produces 10-20 FAA Part 107 certified operators ready for placement
cako_workforce_pipeline.employer_1: Kevin Alexander International LLC dba KAI-Netics (FP)

members.name: James L. Scott, Esq.
members.scope: Patent filings, Form 1023, MONK LLC formation, legal compliance

compliance_rules: NO funds flow from FP to EAF -- IRS private benefit prohibition
MONK IP belongs to MONK LLC only -- never EAF or FP property
NDAA fleet segregation -- Green UAS never mixed with non-NDAA hardware on government contracts
Red team exercises require written authorization and legal review before execution
EAF compliance always beats FP efficiency when they conflict
DJI M350 RTK for private sector only -- never on government contract
Fleet 3 show hardware -- private entertainment only, never government contracts
Fleet 4 FPV -- private EAF property and contracted private-sector red team only
NOTHING leaves the platform outwardly without Kevin explicit approval -- no emails sent, no applications filed, no outreach dispatched without Kevin WhatsApp approval

deadlines.notes: Eligibility check required before Form 1023 filing. Blocks TechSoup, Google for Nonprofits, all 501c3-dependent programs.
Deadline passed -- assess if still open with program contact.
Provisional patent filing overdue. IP protection window at risk.
Requires 501c3 determination letter. Blocked until Form 1023 approved.
Submitted April 17 -- Kevin confirming status. Gates all federal contracts and SBIR.
Primary blocking item for all grant funding. Filing at pay.gov. $275 (1023-EZ) or $600 (full 1023). Eligibility check is prerequisite.
First dollar opportunity. Kevin's CISSP + Part 107 qualifies for compliance consulting. No drone required. $5K-30K per engagement.

deadlines.item: Techstars AI Health Baltimore Demo Day
deadlines.notes: M&T Bank Stadium. Register by May 15. techstars.com/accelerators/baltimore-ai-health.
25% match required. CAKO is anchor program. Jordan has LOI draft. $50K-$500K.

MONK.named_for: Kevin's grandson MONK -- the legacy this platform is building
MONK.description: Hardware-anchored trust layer for autonomous drone operations. Cryptographically signed mission authorization, authenticated identity broadcast, tamper-resistant mission evidence.
MONK.status: Prototype v1.0 in development. Provisional patent filing overdue. MONK LLC formation in progress.
MONK.sbir_target: DHS S&T or DoD -- ~$175,000 Phase I. Pending Congressional reauthorization.
MONK.bvlos_relevance: MONK safety architecture is the core of Part 108 compliance case. TSA security requirements in BVLOS NPRM directly favor MONK-equipped fleets.

AKMS.description: Software OS for High-Definition Autonomous Swarms and BVLOS logistics. Data moat compounds with every flight.
AKMS.data_value: 250 drones x show hours x red team exercises = proprietary flight dataset no competitor can replicate

CAKO.description: 12-16 week EAF-run zero-tuition certification: FAA Part 107, Security+, CAPM, PCEP. Trains underserved Baltimore community members as professional drone operators.
CAKO.pipeline: Each cohort adds certified operators to FP Talent Pipeline Agreement and DMV employer network
